SEARCH
Package org.simulator.math.odes.LSODA

Class LSODAIntegrator

java.lang.Object
org.simulator.math.odes.AbstractDESSolver
org.simulator.math.odes.AdaptiveStepsizeIntegrator
org.simulator.math.odes.LSODA.LSODAIntegrator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable, org.apache.commons.math.ode.events.EventHandler, DelayValueHolder, DESSolver
public class LSODAIntegrator extends AdaptiveStepsizeIntegrator

    • computeChange

      public double[] computeChange(DESystem DES, double[] y2, double time, double stepSize, double[] change, boolean steadyState) throws org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException

      Specified by:
      computeChange in class AbstractDESSolver
      Parameters:
      DES - The system to be simulated.
      y2 - The current state of the system.
      time - The current simulation time.
      stepSize - The current integration step size.
      change - The vector for the resulting change of the system.
      steadyState -
      Returns:
      The change.
      Throws:
      org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException

    • intdy

      public static int intdy(LSODAContext ctx, double t, int k, double[] dky)
      Computes the k-th derivative of the solution at a given time t (t).

      This method evaluates the interpolating polynomial (or its derivatives) for the dependent variables at the given time t, using the history data stored in the LSODACommon object. The result is returned in the arrray dky, which represents the k-th derivative of y(t). The function performs bounds checking on both k and invalid input: '{@' code t} to ensure interpolation is valid. The interpolation is based on Newton-form divided differences and applies a nested multiplication scheme to evaluate the polynomial. The result is scaled appropriately if k > 0.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODAContext object containing the integration state, history arrays and control parameters
      t - the time at which interpolation is to be performed (must lie within the last time interval)
      k - the order of the derivative to be computed (must be in range 0 ≤ k ≤ nq)
      dky - the output array into which the k-th derivative of y(t) will be written (must have at least length neq + 1)
      Returns:
      0 if successful
    • -1 if k is out of range
    • -2 if t is outside the valid interpolation interval

    • intdyReturn

      public int intdyReturn(LSODAContext ctx, double[] y, double[] t, double tout, int itask)

      Parameters:

      Returns:

    • allocMemory

      public boolean allocMemory(int mxords, int mxordn)
      Initializes LSODACommon variabales with suitable values, and set it in LSODAContext
      Parameters:
      mxords - maximum order for stiff method
      mxordn - maximum order for nonstiff method
      Returns:
      boolean Returns true on successful allocation

    • lsodaPrepare

      public boolean lsodaPrepare(LSODAContext ctx, LSODAOptions opt)
      Initializes and prepares an LSODA solver context for integration.

      This function allocates and initializes all memory required for solving a system of ODEs using the LSODA method. It also validates and sets up default solver options if they are not provided explicitly in the LSODAOptions object. It must be called before calling lsoda() for the first time with a given ctx context.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODAContext object
      opt - the LSODAOptions object
      Returns:
      boolean Returns true on successful preparation
      Throws:
      IllegalInputException

    • prepare

      public boolean prepare(org.apache.commons.math.ode.FirstOrderDifferentialEquations system, int ixpr, int itask, int state)
      Initializes and prepares an LSODA solver context for integration.

      This function allocates and initializes all memory required for solving a system of ODEs using the LSODA method. It also validates and sets up default solver options. It must be called before calling solve() for the first time with a given SBMLInterpreter interpreter.

      Parameters:
      system - the DESystem object, will be mostly SBMLInterpreter object
      ixpr - 1 to print messages, 0 to not print messages
      itask - controls task performed by the solver.
      state - tells the solver how to proceed
      Returns:
      bool Returns true on successful preparation
      Throws:
      IllegalInputException

    • prepare

      public boolean prepare(org.apache.commons.math.ode.FirstOrderDifferentialEquations system, int ixpr, int itask, int state, int mxstep)
      Initializes and prepares an LSODA solver context for integration.

      This function allocates and initializes all memory required for solving a system of ODEs using the LSODA method. It also validates and sets up default solver options. It must be called before calling solve() for the first time with a given SBMLInterpreter interpreter.

      Parameters:
      system - the DESystem object, will be mostly SBMLInterpreter object
      ixpr - 1 to print messages, 0 to not print messages
      itask - controls task performed by the solver.
      state - tells the solver how to proceed
      mxstep - maximum numbere of steps allowed
      Returns:
      bool Returns true on successful preparation
      Throws:
      IllegalInputException

    • prepare

      public boolean prepare(org.apache.commons.math.ode.FirstOrderDifferentialEquations system, double hmin, double hmax, int ixpr, int itask, int state)
      Initializes and prepares an LSODA solver context for integration.

      This function allocates and initializes all memory required for solving a system of ODEs using the LSODA method. It also validates and sets up default solver options. It must be called before calling solve() for the first time with a given SBMLInterpreter interpreter.

      Parameters:
      system - the DESystem object, will be mostly SBMLInterpreter object
      hmin - minimum step size
      hmax - maximum step size
      ixpr - 1 to print messages, 0 to not print messages
      itask - controls task performed by the solver.
      state - tells the solver how to proceed
      Returns:
      bool Returns true on successful preparation
      Throws:
      IllegalInputException

    • prepare

      public boolean prepare(org.apache.commons.math.ode.FirstOrderDifferentialEquations system, double hmin, double hmax, int ixpr, int itask, int state, int mxstep)
      Initializes and prepares an LSODA solver context for integration.

      This function allocates and initializes all memory required for solving a system of ODEs using the LSODA method. It also validates and sets up default solver options. It must be called before calling solve() for the first time with a given SBMLInterpreter interpreter.

      Parameters:
      system - the DESystem object, will be mostly SBMLInterpreter object
      hmin - minimum step size
      hmax - maximum step size
      ixpr - 1 to print messages, 0 to not print messages
      itask - controls task performed by the solver.
      state - tells the solver how to proceed
      mxstep - the maximum number of steps allowed
      Returns:
      bool Returns true on successful preparation
      Throws:
      IllegalInputException

    • lsodaReset

      public void lsodaReset(LSODAContext ctx)

    • lsodaFree

      public void lsodaFree(LSODAContext ctx)
      Frees the memory of

      JVM manages on its own

      Parameters:
      ctx -

    • ewset

      public static void ewset(double[] ycur, double[] rtol, double[] atol, int neq, LSODACommon common)
      Computes and sets the error weight vector used for controlling local error.

      This method computes a vector (ewt) based on the current estimate ycur, relative tolerance (rtol) and absolute tolerance (atol) with the formula: 

            ewt[i] = 1 / (rtol[i] * |ycur[i]| + atol[i]) for i = 1, ..., neq
      The computed vector is stored in the LSODACommon object.

      Parameters:
      ycur - the current solution vector
      rtol - the relative tolerance vector
      atol - the absolute tolerance vector
      neq - the number of equations in the system
      common - the LSODACommon object holding the error weight vector and other shared data

    • vmnorm

      public static double vmnorm(int n, double[] v, double[] w)
      Computes the weighted maximum norm of a vector v of length n. The norm is computed using the formula:

      vmnorm = max(i=0,...,n) (|v[i]| * w[i])

      where 'w' is a weight vector of the same length as 'v'.
      Parameters:
      n - the length of the vector v (index range: 1 to n)
      v - the vector whose weighted max-norm is to be computed
      w - the weight vector of the same length as v
      Returns:
      the computed weightes max-norm of the vector

    • cfode

      public static void cfode(LSODAContext ctx, int meth)
      Initializes method-specific coefficients used in the LSODA algorithm.

      This method pre-computes and fills the coefficient tables (elco and tesco) based on the chosen integration method. These coefficient tables are required for evaluating predictor and corrector formulas in the Adams or BDF methods. For Adams method (meth == 1), the fucntion calculates:

      • elco[nq][*]: Coefficients for the polynomial history in the predictor step
      • tesco[nq][*]: Time-step and error estimation related constants
      For BDF method (meth != 1), it computes a simpler version of the same tables for use in the BDF formula.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODAContext object containing integration parameters, method state and history arrays
      meth - the integration method identifiert (1 for Adams, 2 for BDF)

    • scaleh

      public static void scaleh(LSODAContext ctx, double rh)
      Scales the integration step size and the higher-order method history arrays.

      Used to rescale the step size rh and associated history terms after a change in the integration step size is requested. It ensures numerical stability by scaling the multi-step method history array zh. If the method used is Adams (meth == 1). The function also performs additional checks and restricts the scaling factor (rh) to maintain accuracy.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODAContext containing integration parameters, method state and history arrays
      rh - proposed scaling factor for the integration step size

    • correction

      public static int correction(LSODAContext ctx, double[] y, double pnorm, double[] del, double[] delp, double told, int[] m) throws org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException
      Perform the nonlinear corrector step for the current integration step.

      This method applies the Newton iteration or the functional iteration (depending on the method and solver state) to compute a corrected solution vector y for the current step. The correction is applied using the method defined by miter, and convergence is assessed using the weighted norm of the correction vector del.

      If convergence fails within the maximum number of allwed iterations, a failure is reported. If necessary, the Jacobian is recomputed during the first corrector iteration.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODA context object holding the solver configuration, state, memory arrays and user functions
      y - the working solution vector to be corrected; update in-place with the final corrected solution
      pnorm - the norm of the predicted solution (used in convergence check)
      del - the output parameter (length-1 array) for the weighted norm of the current correction
      delp - the output parameter (length-1 array) for the previous occrection norm (used in convergence rate estimation)
      told - the value of t at the beginning of the current step (in case of correction failure)
      m - the output parameter (length-1 array) for the number of correction interations used
      Returns:
      0 if the correction process converges successfully; nonzero if it failed
      Throws:
      org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException

    • solsy

      public static int solsy(LSODAContext ctx, double[] y)
      Solves a linear system arising during the numerical integration process. This method is specifically made for the case where the method indicator ('miter') is equal to 2, which means that the system is solved linearly using LU decomposition with partial pivoting.

      The system solved is of the form> 

            P * L * U * x = y
      where the LU decomposition and pivoting are already performed and stored in the context. The result of the solution replaces the contents of the input vector y.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODAContext holding the system's dimension, method flags, working memory LU factorization and pivot indices (i.e. wm and ipvt).
      y - the right hand side vector of the linear system; upon return, y is overwritten with the solution.
      Returns:
      1, if the system was successfully solved.\n
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the method flag miter is not equal to 2.

    • corfailure

      public static int corfailure(LSODAContext ctx, double told)
      Handles correction failure.

      Updates the solver's internal state when a correction failure occurs during integration. Following key operations are performed:

      • Increment the correction failure counter Ncf.
      • Sets maximum residual factor Rmax to 2 and resets the current time Tn to the provided told value.
      • Adjusts the correction history matrix Yh by computing the difference between successive rows.
      • Checks whether the absolute value of the step size H is less than or equal to a slightly scaled minimum step size (hmin * 1.0001), or if the number of correction failures has reached the maximum allowed MXNCF. If either condition holds, the function returns 2, indicating a fatal failure.
      • If non of the fatal conditions are met, the preconditioner type Ipup is updated based on the current iteration method Miter, and the function returns 1 to signal the failure is recoverable.

      Parameters:
      ctx - Contains the current solver state, internal counters, matrices and configuation parameters.
      told - The previous time value to which the solver reverts upon a correction failure.
      Returns:
      1 if the correction failure is recoverable and the solver can continue; 2 if the failure is fatal.

    • dgesl

      public static void dgesl(double[][] a, int n, int[] ipvt, double[] b, int job)
      Solves a system of linear equations using an LU-factorized matrix.

      This function solvers either A * x = b or AT * x = b, depending on the value of job. The matrix A is assumed to have previously factorized into its LU components, with pivot indices stored in ipvt. The solution is computed in place, with b being overwritte by the solution vector.

      When job == 0, the routine performs a forward substitution followed by a back substiution to solve A * x = b. In the forward phase, a dot product (using ddot()) is used to add contribution from previous vectors, and the algorithm checks for singular or nearly singular pivot elements. In the back substitution phase, additional dot product computations are performed and pivot interchages are applied as per ipvt.

      When job != 0 (will typicall be 1), the function solves the transpoed system AT * x = b. This method uses temporary array copies to simulate the pass-by-reference behavior of the original C implementation. It applies pivot interchanges and performs scaled vector updates (vie daxpy()).

      Parameters:
      a - The LU-factorized matrix of order n, stored as a 2D array with 1-based indexing (index 0 is unused).
      n - The order of the matrix a.
      ipvt - The pivot indices resulting from the LU factorization; ipvt indicates the pivot row for column k.
      b - The right-hand side vector; on output, b is overwritten with the solution vector.
      job - Indicates the system to be solved:
      • job == 0 for solving A * x = b
      • job != 0 for solving AT * x = b

    • daxpy

      public static void daxpy(int n, double da, double[] dx, int incx, double[] dy, int incy, int offsetX, int offsetY)
      Performs a constant times vector plus vector operation (AXPY) on double precision arrays.

      This function computes the operation: 

            dy = da * dx + dy
      where dx and dy are vectors and da is a scalar multiplier. The computation is performed on n elements of the vectors, with the the ability to account for non-unit increments in accessing elements from both dx and dy (set by incx and incy).

      The function first checks if the number of elements (n) is negative or if the scalar da is zero; in both cases, no operation is performed and the functino terminates.

      The function is set to handle several cases:

      • If incx differs from incy or if incy invalid input: '<'= 1, the function computes effective indices for the dx and dy arrays (adjusting for negative increments if necessary) and perform the AXPY operation in a simple loop.
      • If the increment for dx is 1, optimized looping is applied. In this case, the function first processes any remaining elements (n % 4) individually, and the updates the remaining elements in blocks of four to enhance performance.
      • For any other cases, a default iteration over the vector elements is used with offsetX and offsetY as starting iteration index in dx and dy respectively.

      Parameters:
      n - The number of elements in the vectors to be processed; if n is less than 0, no operation will be completed.
      da - The scalar multiplier applied to each element of dx.
      dx - The vector whose elements are scaled by da and then added to the corresponding elements in dy.
      incx - The increment for accessing successive elements of dx.
      dy - The destination vector that is updated in place with the result of the AXPY operation.
      incy - The increment for accessing successive elements of dy.
      offsetX - The start position for dx
      offsetY - The start position for dy

    • ddot

      public static double ddot(int n, double[] dx, double[] dy, int incx, int incy)
      Computes the dot product of two vectors dx and dy of length n, with given increments incx and incy. The dot product is computed using the formula: 
          dotprod = sum(i=0,...,n-1) dx[start_x + i * incx] * dy[start_y + i * incy]
      where `start_x` and `start_y` depend on the increment direction.
      Parameters:
      n - the number of elements to include in the dot product computation
      dx - the first vector
      dy - the second vector
      incx - the increment for accessing elements in dx
      incy - the increment for accessing elements in dy
      Returns:
      the computed dot product of the two vectors
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if dx or dy are too small for the given increments

    • prja

      public static int prja(LSODAContext ctx, double[] y) throws org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException
      Approximates and factorizes the Jacobian matrix used for preconditioning in the ODE solver.

      This function computes a finite difference approximation to the Jacobian matrix for the current state of the ODE system. It perturbs the state vector y and evaluates the system function to estimate the directional derivatives. The resulting differences, scaled by the step size, tolerance and a computed factor, are used to update the weighted difference matrix Wm. The matrix is then modified by utilizing its diagonal elements and factorized via LU decomposition (by calling dgefa()). This makes it ready for use as a preconditioner in the iterative solution process.

      The algorithm is executed only if the iteration method miter = 2. In cases where this is not true, an error is logged and the function returns a failure status. Additionally, counters for Jacobian evaluations (nje) and function evaluations (nfe) are updated accordingly.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODA context containing solver state, common data, tolerances (abosulte and relative), and ODE function evaluation informationthe LSODA context containing solver state, common data, tolerances (abosulte and relative), and ODE function evaluation information
      y - the state vector of the ODE system that is used to compute the finite differences for Jacobian approximation
      Returns:
      1: if the Jacobian approximation and subsequent matrix factorization succeed; 2: 0 if an error occurs (e.g., an unexpected iteration method)
      Throws:
      org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException

    • dgefa

      public static void dgefa(double[][] a, int n, int[] ipvt, int[] info)
      Perform an LU factorization of a square matrix using Gaussian elimination **with** partial pivoting.

      This function factorizes the matrix a in place into a product of a lower triangular matrix L and an upper triangular matrix U (with the unit diagonal of L bein implicit). The factorization is computed per Gaussian elimination with partial pivoting, i.e. for each column the pivot is selected based on the maximum absolute value in the current column segment (utilizing idamax())

      For each column index k from 1 to n - 1, the algorithm perform the following steps:

      1. A helper column vector is constructed from a[k..n][k] to replicate pointer logic of the original C code, or better said, to simulate pass-by-reference from the C code. The pivot index is then determined by idamax()
      2. The pivot index is stored in the ipvt array. If the pivot element is zero, which indicates that the matrix is singular, the rountine sets info[0] to k and terminates.
      3. If the pivot is not already in position k, the corresponding rows are swapped.
      4. The pivot row is scaled by computing a factor of (-1) / a[k][k] (using dscal()). The submatrix is then updaated by calling on daxpy.

      Parameters:
      a - The square matrix to be factorized, stored as a 2D array; it is modified in place to contain the L and U factors. Note: the implementation uses 1-based indexing as in the original C code!
      n - The order of the matrix (number of rows and columns).
      ipvt - An integer array in which the pivot indices are recorded; On output, ipvt[k] holds the index of the pivot element for column k.
      info - An integer array of length >= 1 used as an output flag; info[0] is set to 0 if the factorization is successful. If a zero pivot is encountered at column k, info[0] is set to k and the routine terminates.

    • fnorm

      public static double fnorm(int n, double[][] a, double[] w)
      Computes the Frobenius norm of an n x n matrix a, which is consistent with the weighted max-norm on vectors. The norm is calculated using the formula: 
          fnorm = max(i=1,...,n) (w[i] * sum(j=1,...,n) fabs(a[i][j]) / w[j])
      where 'w' is a weight vector.
      Parameters:
      n - the size of the (square) matrix
      a - the n x n matrix whose norm is to be calculated
      w - the weight vector of length n used for normalization
      Returns:
      the computed weighted norm of the matrix

    • idamax

      public static int idamax(int n, double[] dx, int incx)
      Finds the index of the element with the maxiumum absolute value in the vector dx.

      The method scans a vector of double precision numbers over n elements using a specified increment incx. It returns the 1-indexed position of the element whose absolute value is the largest. If n is less than or equal to 0, the method returns 0. If n is 1 or if incx is not positive, the method returns 1 (i.e. the first element is the maximum by default). When incx is not equal to 1, the method steps through the vector by incx increments; otherwise, it processes the vector sequentially for better performance.

      Parameters:
      n - the number of elements in dx to be examined
      dx - the input vector of double values
      incx - the increment for iterating through elements in dx
      Returns:
      the 1-indexed position of the element with the larges absolute value, of 0 if n is less that or equal to 0

    • dscal

      public static void dscal(int n, double da, int incx, double[] dx)
      Scales a vector by a constant factor.

      The function multiplies n elements of the input vector dx by the scalar da. The elements to be scaled are iterated using incx.

      When incx is not equal to 1, the function iterates over the vector with steps of size incx. If incx = 1, the remaining elements (m = n % 5) are first scaled individually, and then the remaining elements are processed in blocks of five.

      Parameters:
      n - The number of elements in the vector dx to be scaled; if n invalid input: '<'= 0, no operation is performed and the function terminates.
      da - The scalar value by which each element of dx is multiplied.
      incx - The increment by which elements in the vector dx are accessed.
      dx - The vector to be scaled; the selected elements are modified in place.

    • methodSwitch

      public static void methodSwitch(LSODAContext ctx, double dsm, double pnorm, double[] rh)
      handles switching between nonstiff (Adams) and stiff (BDF) methods

      Compares estimated performance and stability of the current method meth with the alternative method.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODA context containing solver state, common data, options data and number of equations
      dsm - normalized local-error estimate at current order
      pnorm - predicted error norm
      rh - output: the factor by which to multiply the current stepsize h

    • orderSwitch

      public static int orderSwitch(LSODAContext ctx, double rhup, double dsm, double[] rh, int kflag, int maxord)
      detects whether to change the order of current method

      This function is called by stoda after each successful or failed step to evaluate whether to adjust the multistep method's order (nq) and/or the integration step size (h) based on local error estimates, stored derivative history, and stability constraints.

      It computes three candidate step-size factors:

      • rhdn : for decreasing order (nq - 1)
      • rhsm : for keeping the same order (nq)
      • rhup : for increasing order (nq + 1)
      and then chooses the order yielding the largest stable step size. If order is increased, the corresponding additional backward difference yh[nq+1] is computed using the most recent corrector vector, acor.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the LSODA context containing solver state, common data, options data and number of equations
      rhup - ideal stepsize factor, if one step up in order
      dsm - normalized local-error estimate at current order
      rh - output: the factor by which to multiply the current stepsize h
      kflag - flag from previous step, negative if prior error test or correction failure
      maxord - maximum allowed order in current family
      Returns:
      0: Keep the same stepsize and order 1: Change in stepsize but same order 2: Change in both stepsize and order

    • findDerivatives

      public static double[] findDerivatives(org.apache.commons.math.ode.FirstOrderDifferentialEquations odeSystem, double t, double[] y) throws org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException
      Throws:
      org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException

    • lsoda

      public int lsoda(LSODAContext ctx, double[] y, double[] t, double tout) throws org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException
      Main lsoda driver function for integrating a system of ODEs This function numerically solves an initial value problem (IVP) of the form: 
           dy/dt = f(t, y)
      for a given initial condition y(t0) = y0, from time t towards tout, using the LSODA algorithm.
      Parameters:
      ctx - LSODAContext object containing the problem setup, memory workspaces, and ODE system definition.
      y - the array of dependent variables of size `neq`.
      t - array of unit size containing the current timestep
      tout - the target output time for the integration.
      Returns:
      int Status code (same as ctx->state):
      • 2: Success. Result found at time tout.
      Negative values indicate failure:
      • -1: Too much work attempted.
      • -2: Too much accuracy requested (rtol/atol too small).
      • -3: Illegal input detected.
      • -4: Repeated local error test failures.
      • -5: Repeated convergence failures.
      • -6: Error weight ewt[i] became zero.
      • -7: Insufficient workspace.
      Throws:
      org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException

    • lsoda

      public int lsoda(double[] y, double[] t, double tout) throws org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException
      Parameters:
      y -
      t -
      tout -
      Returns:
      Throws:
      org.apache.commons.math.ode.DerivativeException
